10.response: {"Q0":"I assumed correctly so it didn't really affect me, but when the astronaut left I was unsure if they would ever come back and if they did would I get some kind of message stating such or would it just happen in a following round."}

11.response: {"Q0":"I would assume how people value the chance of something happening compared to the potential reward it could generate."}

0.response: {"Q0":"I don't think my strategy was more developed than simply earning as much gold as I could."}

9.response: {"Q0":"I guess I was probably more focused on galaxy choices as you had less time to react to whether it was right or left."}

1.response: {"Q0":"I didn't make any conscious changes to my strategy while playing, unless you count not digging on a planet after the astronaut left as a change in strategy."}

8.response: {"Q0":"I generally overvalue success rate to payout I would say."}

3.response: {"Q0":"I only saw the message once and it was for the red planet."}
